params.name: Chicago Pile-1
params.description:
On Dec 2, 1942 AD, Chicago Pile-1 was successfully initiated under the leadership of physicist Enrico Fermi.
Chicago Pile-1 was an experimental nuclear reactor and part of the Manhattan Project, whose main purpose was to demonstrate the chain reaction model of nuclear fission and lay the groundwork for the development of nuclear weapons. Despite its rough appearance, resembling a sphere made of graphite blocks and uranium fuel, it successfully operated and became the world's first controlled …
